rulings.Case Title: MARIA CHAVEZ VS. F&M OBERTI INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 23, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT F&M OBERTI INC, MARCELLA OBERTI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion for summary judgment granted. Defendant shifts the burden and plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue of fact. Bluer testimony lacks foundation and does not establish a dangerous condition. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. THE LOUGH ROAD, INC., AN OREGON CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendants Pier 39 Limited Partnership And Pier Restaurants Lp Demurrer To 2nd Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 23, 2009, line 11, Cross-Defendants Pier 39 limited partnership and Pier restaurants LP demurrer to second amended cross-complaint (transferred from Department 301, per challenge to Judge Busch). Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth and eighth causes of action. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action to designate as "unjust enrichment" and ninth and tenth causes of action to specifically plead how Pier 39 is responsible for the leakage or its inactions in failing to remedy the leakage. Cross-complainants to correct typographical errors in paragraphs 19, and 34 and specify how Rodney Lough, Jr. is entitled to damages. Pier 39's motion to dismiss Lough, Inc. is granted without prejudice, pending Lough, Inc.'s registration to do business in the State of California. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND LAM VS. NHU THUY NGUYEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 23, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT NHU THUY NGUYEN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend. No opposition filed. Plaintiff must attach a copy of the contract or plead whether the contract is oral, written, or is implied by conduct. CCP section 430.10 (e)&(g). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND LAM VS. NHU THUY NGUYEN et al
